title: senecio atrofuscus
intro: Senecio atrofuscus, also known as Black Thistle or Ragwort, is a biennial plant native to Europe and parts of Asia, known for its distinctive dark purple or black flowers and bitter-tasting leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Senecio atrofuscus include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, Senecio atrofuscus is often used as a tincture, dried herb, or infused oil, and can be consumed as a tea or applied topically to affected areas.
